entries.title: Gondor And Rohan Have Distinct Assumptions

context.label: Gondor And Rohan Have Distinct Assumptions

content.fact: Gondor and Rohan are allied realms with different political cultures, military habits, histories, and social language.
content.injection: Keep Gondor citadel, steward, lineage, and long-war oriented; keep Rohan hall, oath, horse, and honor oriented.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.
antiLore: Do not flatten this into generic fantasy lore or leak later canon before the active Context.

entries.title: Orcs Serve Through Fear Not Fellowship

context.label: Orcs Serve Through Fear Not Fellowship

content.fact: Orc forces are driven by fear, domination, cruelty, appetite, and factional coercion rather than loyal fellowship.
content.injection: Orc groups can be dangerous without being cohesive; rivalry and fear of masters shape behavior.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.
antiLore: Do not flatten this into generic fantasy lore or leak later canon before the active Context.

entries.title: Rangers Protect Lands That Distrust Them

context.label: Rangers Protect Lands That Distrust Them

content.fact: Rangers guard Eriador from unseen dangers while many ordinary people mistrust or dismiss them.
content.injection: Let Rangers carry quiet duty and poor public reputation together; protection does not guarantee gratitude.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.
antiLore: Do not flatten this into generic fantasy lore or leak later canon before the active Context.

content.fact: Sauron’s power grows through fear, pride, despair, greed, and divided loyalties as much as armies.
content.injection: Use corruption and despair as strategic tools where leaders or allies already distrust one another.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.
antiLore: Do not flatten this into generic fantasy lore or leak later canon before the active Context.

entries.title: The Free Peoples Lack Modern Coordination

context.label: The Free Peoples Lack Modern Coordination

content.fact: The Free Peoples are divided by distance, history, fear, pride, and limited communication.
content.injection: Councils, messengers, old alliances, and personal trust are needed; there is no centralized modern coalition.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.
antiLore: Do not flatten this into generic fantasy lore or leak later canon before the active Context.

content.fact: War affects civilians through fear, occupation, hunger, conscription, displacement, suspicion, and damaged local order.
content.injection: Let war pressure reach farms, roads, halls, households, and healers, not just battle lines.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.
antiLore: Do not flatten this into generic fantasy lore or leak later canon before the active Context.

entries.title: Mordor Acts Through Fear Spies And Servants

context.label: Mordor's reach during the War of the Ring

content.fact: Mordor's power reaches beyond its borders through fear, armies, spies, servants, rumors, and the will of Sauron.
content.injection: Show its reach through fear, surveillance, servants, military pressure, and corruption rather than only through open armies.
constraints: Mordor's reach should feel oppressive but not omniscient.
Sauron's servants can create suspicion even far from Mordor.
antiLore: Do not make Sauron personally aware of every minor scene by default.
Do not reduce Mordor's influence to battlefield force only.

sourceInfo.title: The Lord of the Rings
sourceInfo.notes: Faction behavior constraint. Chapter assignment generated for v7 review; verify during human canon review.

entries.title: Free Peoples Are Not Unified By Default
